description: A mill is equal to $1.00 of tax for each $1,000 of assessment. To calculate the property tax, multiply the assessment of the property by the mill rate and divide by 1,000. For example, a property with an assessed value of $50,000 located in a municipality with a mill rate of 20 mills would have a property tax bill of $1,000 per year. The rates for each year are based on the grand list from two years prior. For example, the mill rates for the 2021 fiscal year are based off the 2019 grand list. St

columns.description: Heroin DC are those cases in which heroin was listed on the death certificate (DC). Since heroin breaks down quickly, sometimes medical examiners will list morphine (not heroin) on the DC. On retrospective review, some of the DCs marked as morphine represented heroin. Those were tracked in the heroin column.

description: A listing of each accidental death associated with drug overdose in Connecticut from 2012 to 2024. A "Y" value under the different substance columns indicates that particular substance was detected. Data are derived from an investigation by the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner which includes the toxicity report, death certificate, as well as a scene investigation. name: Spill Incidents from January 1, 1996 to June 30, 2022
provider: Department of Energy & Environmental Protection, Emergency Response and Spill Prevention Division

columns.name: Representing
columns.description: The entity reporting the spill incident

columns.name: Responsibile Party/Discharger
columns.description: The person or entity identified as causing the spill incident

columns.name: Responsible Party Address
columns.description: The address of the individual or entity identified as causing the spill incident

columns.name: Responsible Party Town
columns.description: The town associated with the address of the person or entity identified as causing the spill incident

columns.name: Total Quantity Feet
columns.description: Amount in Cubic Feet of substance spilled or found --  “0” indicates indicates zero or can indicate an unknown amount. Note that the quantity can include the volume of contaminated soil or water combined with the spilled substance. Standard data qualifiers are symbols for greater than (>), less than (<), and approximate (~), though other symbols may be present, either intentionally or as typographic errors.

columns.name: Responsible Party State
columns.description: The state associated with the address of the person or entity identified as causing the spill incident

columns.name: Responsible Party Zip
columns.description: The postal zip code associated with the address of the person or entity identified as causing the spill incident.

columns.name: Responsible Party Accepts Responsibility (Y/N)
columns.description: If known at the time of the incident report, the name of the person or entity who accepts responsibility for the release.

columns.name: Release Substance
columns.description: Common name or category of spilled substance or material

columns.name: Total Quantity Gallons
columns.description: Amount in Gallons of substance spilled or found --  “0” indicates indicates zero or can indicate an unknown amount. Note that the quantity can include the volume of contaminated soil or water combined with the spilled substance. Standard data qualifiers are symbols for greater than (>), less than (<), and approximate (~), though other symbols may be present, either intentionally or as typographic errors.

columns.name: Total Quantity Yards
columns.description: Amount in Cubic Yards of substance spilled or found -- “0” indicates indicates zero or can indicate an unknown amount. Note that the quantity can include the volume of contaminated soil or water combined with the spilled substance. Standard data qualifiers are symbols for greater than (>), less than (<), and approximate (~), though other symbols may be present, either intentionally or as typographic errors.

columns.name: Case No.
columns.description: The unique administrative case filing number -- the first two to four digits represent the year in which the incident was reported

columns.name: Assigned to
columns.description: The Emergency Response Coordinator or Supervisor initially assigned to respond to the incident

columns.name: Year
columns.description: The year the incident was reported

columns.name: Date Reported Time Reported
columns.description: The date and time the spill incident information was received

columns.name: Release date and time
columns.description: The date and time the spill incident occurred (sometimes estimated)

columns.name: Town of Release
columns.description: The town in which the release occurred -- the name is the official Connecticut town name, not the village or borough name

columns.name: State of Release
columns.description: The state is generally Connecticut, though reports of spills in neighboring states may be reported if occurring near Connecticut, particularly in water bodies near state borders

columns.name: Status
columns.description: The administrative status of the agency’s report, with “closed” indicating either that a report has been issued if a response occurred, or that no emergency response occurred.

columns.name: Release Type
columns.description: The categories of the reported substance(s) released:  petroleum, chemical, dialect,  hazardous waste, sewage related, biomedical, gas emission, other, or not specified.

columns.name: Media Info

description: Syndromic surveillance provides public health officials with a timely system for detecting, understanding, and monitoring health events. Syndromic data can serve as an early warning system for public health concerns such as influenza and COVID-19 outbreaks. These data represent the percent of emergency department and urgent care visits related to Influenza

name: 2024-2025 Kindergarten Immunization Rates by School

columns.name: MMR
columns.description: Percentage of students with at least 2 doses of MMR (measles, mumps, and rubella) vaccine separated by at least 28 days, with the 1st dose on or after their 1st birthday.

columns.name: Varicella
columns.description: Percentage of students with at least 2 doses of varicella vaccine separated by at least 28 days, with the 1st dose on or after their 1st birthday, or a reliable history of chickenpox disease.

columns.name: HepA
columns.description: Percentage of students with at least 2 doses of hepatitis A vaccine, given a minimum of six calendar months apart, with the 1st dose on or after their 1st birthday.

columns.name: Ex_Rel
columns.description: Percentage of students with a religious exemption. This value is the number of students with a religious exemption divided by the total number of students.

columns.name: Ex_Tot
columns.description: Percentage of students with any exemption. This value is the number of students with any exemption divided by the total number of students.

columns.name: Ex_Med
columns.description: Percentage of students with a medical exemption. This value is the number of students with a medical exemption divided by the total number of students.

columns.name: Zipcode
columns.description: displays school zip code as reported

columns.name: HepB
columns.description: Percentage of students with at least 3 doses of hepatitis B vaccine, with the last dose on or after 24 weeks of age.

columns.name: All
columns.description: Percentage of students with all vaccine series.

columns.name: School Type
columns.description: displays whether the school is public or private

columns.name: Polio
columns.description: Percentage of students with at least 3 doses of inactivated polio vaccine, with the last dose on or after their 4th birthday.

columns.name: DTaP
columns.description: Percentage of students with at least 4 doses of DTaP (diphtheria, tetanus, and acellular pertussis) vaccine, with the last dose on or after their 4th birthday.

description: This dataset provides access to Qualified Census Tracts (QCTs) in Connecticut to assist in administration of American Rescue Plan (ARP) funds. The Secretary of HUD must designate QCTs, which are areas where either 50 percent or more of the households have an income less than 60 percent of the AMGI for such year or have a poverty rate of at least 25 percent. columns.description: The tax dollars due on personal property on the grand list. To calculate this amount, the final net grand list for personal property is to multiplied by the mill rate(s).

columns.description: The tax dollars due on real property on the grand list. To calculate this amount, the final net grand list for real property is  multiplied by the mill rate(s).

columns.name: icu_patients_confirmed_influenza_7_day_coverage
columns.description: [FAQ - 35] Number of times in the 7-day period that the facility reported patients (all ages) currently hospitalized in a designated ICU bed with laboratory-confirmed influenza.

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_one_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 43] The current number reported of healthcare personnel who have received at least one dose of COVID-19 vaccination that is administered in a multi-dose series. This field is meant to represent those who have begun but not completed the vaccination process. Does not include those who received a single-dose vaccine in this field. (Collected on Wednesdays only)

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_none_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 42] The number reported of healthcare personnel who have not yet received a single vaccine dose. (Collected on Wednesdays only)

columns.description: [FAQ - 41] The number reported of COVID-19 vaccination doses administered in the previous week regardless of whether it is a first or second dose in a series. (Collected on Wednesdays only)

columns.name: hhs_id
columns.description: As a part of an effort to improve the granularity of spatial data, unique identifiers (named “HHS IDs” in the datasets) have been assigned to each individual facility. These unique identifiers are provided so data users can reference each individual “brick and mortar” facility that is reporting data to HHS, even in cases when multiple facilities report under the same CMS Certification Number (CCN). Additional datasets and further details related to HHS IDs will be released at a later date.

name: Public Investment Community Index
description: The Office of Policy and Management (OPM) prepares the Public Investment Community (PIC) index not later than July 15 annually, pursuant to §7-545 of the Connecticut General Statutes (CGS). The PIC index measures the relative wealth and need of Connecticut’s towns by ranking them in descending order by their cumulative point allocations for: (1) per capita income; (2) adjusted equalized net grand list per capita; (3) equalized mill rate; (4) per capita aid to children receiving Temporary Family

This dataset lists programs that were funded through LoCIP.

name: Estimates of Statutory Aid to Municipalities
provider: Office of Policy and Management, State Department of Education, Department of Transportation

columns.name: Mashantucket Pequot & Mohegan Fund Grant
columns.description: Town-by-town payment lists for Mashantucket Pequot and Mohegan Fund grants have been established each year since FY 2018. The payment list for FY 2025 grants was established in section 38 of PA 23-204, and the payment lists for FY 2026 and FY 2027 grants were established in section 25 of PA 25-168. The payment list also includes grants to the Paucatuck Eastern Pequot, Schaghticoke, and Golden Hill Paugussett tribes in the amount of $20,000 annually for each tribe.

columns.name: Special Education and Expansion Development
columns.description: Special Education and Expansion Development (SEED) grants are administered by the State Department of Education pursuant to section 7 of PA25-67. Grants are distributed to municipalities within available appropriations beginning in FY 2026 based on special education enrollment and certain weights included in the Education Cost Sharing formula. For FY 2026 and FY 2027, $30 million is appropriated for SEED grants.

columns.name: Education Cost Sharing
columns.description: The State Department of Education administers Education Equalization Grants, or Education Cost Sharing (ECS), pursuant to C.G.S. section 10-262f, section 10-262g, section 10-262h, section 10-262i, and section 10-262j. Each grantee's entitlement is determined by a formula in statute that has been phased in since FY 2018. During the phase-in period, districts have received scheduled increases or decreases to their ECS grants to bring them closer to the grant amount yielded by the statutory formula. Districts with increasing ECS grants during the phase-in period will reach their full formula amounts in FY 2026. Districts with decreasing ECS grants have been held harmless from reductions since FY 2022 and will continue to receive their FY 2025 grant amounts in both FY 2026 and FY 2027. Reported grant amounts for FY 2025 reflect final ECS grants inclusive of prior year adjustments. Grant amounts for FY 2026 and FY 2027 reflect ECS entitlements exclusive of prior year adjustments, which will be applied towards the close of the fiscal year if necessary.

columns.name: Municipal Grants-in-Aid
columns.description: The Office of Policy and Management administers this program for the construction and maintenance of public highways, roads, and bridges pursuant to C.G.S. section 13a-175a. Payment lists for the distribution of grants were established in section 55 of PA 23-205 for FY 2024 and FY 2025, and in section 55 of PA 25-174 for FY 2026 and FY 2027. For FY 2026 and FY 2027, the total bond authorization was increased from $91 million to $150 million, with the $59 million increase distributed to eight municipalities.

columns.name: Town Aid Road
columns.description: The Department of Transportation administers the Town Aid Road Fund grant pursuant to C.G.S. section 13a-175a through section 13a-175e, inclusive, and section 13a-175i. Towns and boroughs use these grants for various purposes, including the construction and maintenance of public highways, roads and bridges. In FY 2024 and FY 2025, funding was distributed via Special Transportation Fund appropriation instead of bond authorizations. Bond funding for the program is resumed in FY 2026.

columns.name: Supplemental Revenue Sharing
columns.description: Beginning in FY 2024, statutory payment lists for Municipal Revenue Sharing Grants and Municipal Stabilization Grants were consolidated into a single payment list for Supplemental Revenue Sharing Grants in section 4-66p as amended by PA 23-204. Funding for Supplemental Revenue Sharing is appropriated from the Municipal Revenue Sharing Fund. Notwithstanding the payment list established in statute, increased payments to eleven municipalities are established for FY 2026 and FY 2027 in section 180 of Public Act 25-174.

columns.name: Adult Education
columns.description: The Adult Education grant is administered by the State Department of Education pursuant to C.G.S. section 10-71 and section 10-71a. Grants to reimburse adult education expenditures are determined on a sliding scale with a percentage ranging from 0% to 65%.

columns.name: Local Capital Improvement (LoCIP)
columns.description: LoCIP grants are administered pursuant to C.G.S. section 7-535 through section 7-538, inclusive, for local capital projects. During the 2023 legislative session, the total bond authorization supporting the program was increased to $45 million in FY 2024 and FY 2025, resulting in a 50% increase to each municipality or borough’s grant. Beginning in FY 2024, LoCIP is distributed as a grant instead of reimbursement payment.

columns.name: Motor Vehicle Tax Reimbursement
columns.description: Motor vehicle tax reimbursements are administered by OPM according to the formula in section 4-66l of the 2024 Supplement to the Connecticut General Statutes to reimburse municipalities and districts for the revenue losses caused by the 32.46 mill cap on motor vehicle property tax rates. Funding for Motor Vehicle Tax Reimbursements is appropriated from the Municipal Revenue Sharing Fund.

columns.name: Tiered PILOT
columns.description: Tiered Payment-in-Lieu of Taxes (PILOT) is administered according to section 12-18b as of the 2024 Supplement to the Connecticut General Statutes to reimburse municipalities for revenue losses due to exemptions on: (1) state-owned property, and (2) certain private, tax-exempt property. Reimbursements are calculated according to a statutory formula that accounts for each municipality's Equalized Net Grand List Per Capita (ENGLPC), as well as other variables. During the 2023 legislative session, reimbursement percentages for all three tiers were increased by 3%, from 30%/40%/50% to 33%/43%/53%, beginning in FY 2024. Total funding for the program was increased by $19.0 million to accommodate the increased reimbursement percentages. Beginning in FY 2026, special payments to certain towns are reallocated from the Tiered PILOT formula to Supplemental Revenue Sharing. Funding for Tiered PILOT is appropriated from the Municipal Revenue Sharing Fund.

description: Beginning with tax year ("TY") 2021, this data asset will be the new home for the "Individual Income Tax Reports" which were previously uploaded to the Department of Revenue Services ("DRS") page of the state website. Connecticut imposes a tax on income earned by resident individuals, trusts, and estates. Nonresidents and part-year resident individuals, trusts, and estates are also subject to the tax on income derived from or connected with sources within the State.

columns.description: A district/school is identified as having an achievement gap if the size of its index score gap between the High Needs subgroup and the Non-High Needs group (or the ultimate achievement target of 75 if that’s lower) is a significant outlier i.e., at least one standard deviation greater than the statewide gap in any subject area.

columns.name: FinalCategory
columns.description: All schools are placed into one of five categories. Categories 4 and 5 represent those identified for comprehensive support (TURNAROUND) or targeted support (FOCUS). The remaining schools are categorized into either 1, 2 or 3 based on the Accountability Index, achievement/graduation gaps and assessment participation rates.

columns.name: Accountability Index
columns.description: Overall Accountability Index. This is the percentage of possible points earned. It is TotalPoints divided by TotalPossiblePoints.

columns.description: Total Accountability Points Possible. This varies based on grades offered at the school (e.g., points for graduation indicators are not possible for elementary schools).

The SLFRF program, a part of the American Rescue Plan, provided $350 billion to state, local, and Tribal governments across the country to support their response to and recovery from the COVID-19 public health emergency. This dataset includes funds allocated to Connecticut municipalities.

description: This dataset contains aggregate data concerning the number of children who entered DCF placement during a given SFY (July 1 – June 30). These figures are broken out by the DCF Region and Office responsible for the child's care, the child's Race/Ethnicity, and the Placement Type category into which the child was initially placed.

name: COVID-19 Vaccinations by Gender - ARCHIVED
description: NOTE: As of 2/16/2023, this page is no longer being updated. Important change as of June 1, 2022 As of June 1, 2022, we will be using 2020 DPH provisional census estimates* to calculate vaccine coverage percentages by gender at the state level. 2020 estimates will replace the 2019 estimates that have been used. Caution should be taken when making comparisons of percentages calculated using the 2019 and 2020 census estimates since observed difference may result from the shift in the denominator.

name: Toxics Release Inventory Facility List
description: The Toxics Release Inventory (TRI) tracks the management of certain toxic chemicals that may pose a threat to human health and the environment. Certain industrial facilities in the U.S. must report annually how much of each chemical is recycled, combusted for energy recovery, treated for destruction, and disposed of or otherwise released on- and off-site. This information is collectively referred to as production-related waste managed. Descriptions of data terms and field names: https://www.epa.

name: Children Entering DCF Placement: Annual Trend by Age Group
provider: CT Department of Children and Families
description: This dataset contains aggregate data concerning the number of children who entered DCF placement during a given SFY (July 1 – June 30). These figures are broken out by the DCF Region and Office responsible for the child's care, the child's Age Group (based on age at date of entry), and the Placement Type category into which the child was initially placed.

name: Neglected Cemetery Awards
description: C.G.S. Sec. 19a-308b established the Neglected Cemetery Account and authorizes the Office of Policy and Management to make grants to eligible municipalities for the purpose of municipal maintenance of neglected burial grounds and cemeteries, as described in C.G.S. Sec. 19a-308. The program is funded from death certificate fees collected by the Department of Public Health, pursuant to C.G.S. §7-74(b)(2).

name: Cannabis Retail Products Sold by Product Type
description: This data set contains preliminary retail sales data for cannabis and cannabis products by product type in the adult-use cannabis and medical marijuana markets based on total sales data. The data reported is compiled at specific points in time and only captures data current at the time the report is generated. The monthly data set captures retail cannabis sales from the first through the last day of the month. Data values may be updated and change over time as updates occur. Accordingly, monthly

name: Statewide Immunizations and Exemption Rates by School Year, Grade, Vaccine, and School Type

columns.name: Percentage Non-Compliant
columns.description: Percentage of students who have not met school entry requirements, either by having received the required vaccines or having a valid exemption.

columns.description: displays number of schools for which data was reported

columns.name: Total Enrollment Count
columns.description: displays number of students enrolled by reporting schools

columns.name: Total Vaccinated Count
columns.description: displays number of students vaccinated. (Any blank field mean that this data is not available at this time.)

columns.name: Percentage Vaccinated
columns.description: Percentage of students vaccinated. This value is the number of students with the required number of doses of a given vaccine divided by the total number of students.

columns.description: displays number of pre-kindergarten students with a religious influenza only exemption. (Any blank field means that this data is not applicable.)

columns.description: Percentage of students with a religious exemption. This value is the number of students with a religious exemption divided by the total number of students.

columns.description: displays number of students with a medical exemption

columns.name: Percentage Compliant
columns.description: Percentage of students who have met school entry requirements, either by having received the required vaccines or having a valid exemption. columns.name: Grade
columns.description: displays grades for which vaccine data are collected

columns.name: School Type

columns.description: displays number of students with a religious exemption

columns.description: Percentage of students with a medical exemption. This value is the number of students with a medical exemption divided by the total number of students.

columns.name: Vaccine Series
columns.description: displays each school-entry mandated vaccine series collected (See meta data for details)

description: The annual pre-kindergarten (pre-K) through 12th grade school immunization survey collects school-level, grade-specific data on vaccine coverage and exemptions. The survey collects vaccination and exemption status data on children who entered the school system on or before a specified date during the fall semester. Individual vaccine information on each student is not collected. This table shows the statewide vaccination exemptions (medical and religious) and percentage vaccinated and compliant

columns.name: Grid Edge
columns.description: Whether or not the project was installed at a "grid edge" location. Customers on the Grid Edge are defined as (1) the top ten percent of circuits with the highest number of outages per customer during major storms since July 1, 2012, and (2) the top ten percent of circuits with the longest outages due to major storms since July 1, 2012. A map of circuits that meet grid edge criteria can be found on the Program’s website.

description: The Energy Storage Solutions program is an incentive program designed to help Eversource and United Illuminating customers install battery energy storage systems for their home or business. The program helps lower the cost of buying a battery by providing upfront and performance incentives. This incentive program is overseen by the Public Utilities Regulatory Authority (PURA), is paid for by electric ratepayers, and is administered by the Connecticut Green Bank, Eversource, and UI. This dataset

provider: Department of Children and Families, Office for Research and Evaluation

columns.name: DCF_SOLNIT
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is either the Solnit Center North Campus (formerly CT Children's Place) or Solnit Center South Campus (formerly Riverview Psychiatric Hospital)

columns.name: GROUP_HOME
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of Group Home, which includes Maternity, Preparing Adolescents for Self-Sufficiency (PASS), Specialized or Therapeutic Group Home settings

columns.name: RESIDENTIAL
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is a Residential Treatment Center

columns.name: Demographic
columns.description: Child’s Race/Ethnicity (HISPANIC = Hispanic, Any Race, BLACK = Non-Hispanic, Black/African-American, OTHER = Non-Hispanic, Other Race, WHITE = Non-Hispanic, White/Caucasian)

columns.name: Month_in_Care
columns.description: Observation Date; date the child was residing in an open DCF placement

columns.name: DCF_Region
columns.description: DCF Region responsible for child's case

columns.name: FOSTER_CARE
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of DCF "Core" foster home (recruited, trained, licensed and supported by DCF directly)

columns.name: DCF_CJTS
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is the CT Juvenile Training School

columns.name: HOSPITAL
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is in a Hospital setting for either medical or psychiatric reasons

columns.name: THERAPEUTIC_FOSTER_CARE
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of Therapeutic foster home (recruited, trained, and supported by a Child-Placing Agency on contract with DCF)

columns.name: PDC_SAFE_HOME
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is either a Permanency Diagnostic Center (not utilized since 2011), or a Safe Home (a temporary congregate care service that is a homelike setting providing immediate, 24-hour care for children ages 6-13, with some exceptions to age made to keep sibling groups together)

columns.name: SHELTER
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of Shelter (a temporary congregate care program that provides short-term care, assessment and a range of clinical and nursing services)

columns.name: INDEPENDENT_LIVING
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of supervised but independent living setting, including Supportive Work, Education and Transition Programs (SWET), or Community Housing Apartment Programs (CHAP)

columns.name: SPECIAL_STUDY
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is a Special Study foster home (provider is connected to the child in some fashion, but is not a member of the child's extended family;  in most cases providers are recruited, trained, licensed and supported by DCF directly, though some provide Therapeutic Foster Care but for reporting purposes are included in the Special Study category)

columns.name: RELATIVE_CARE
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is a Relative Foster home (provider is a member of the child's extended family; in most cases providers are recruited, trained, licensed and supported by DCF directly, though some provide Therapeutic Foster Care but for reporting purposes are included in the Relative Care category)

columns.name: DCF_Office
columns.description: DCF Office responsible for child's case

columns.name: DCF_HIGHMEADOWS
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is the High Meadows facility (previously run by DCF and closed in 2009)

columns.name: TOTAL_CIP
columns.description: Total number of children in placement on the Month In Care date

columns.name: Data_as_of
columns.description: Date the data was pulled from the database

description: This dataset contains aggregate data concerning the number of unique children placed in open DCF placements on the observation date (July 1st each year). These figures are broken out by the DCF Region and Office responsible for the child's care, the child's Race/Ethnicity, whether the placement setting is in or out-of-state, and by the categorical Placement Type in which the child is residing on the observation date.

description: Small business transactions and revenue data aggregated from several credit card processors, collected by Womply and compiled by Opportunity Insights. columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is either a Permanency Diagnostic Center (not utilized since 2011), or a Safe Home (a temporary congregate care service that is a homelike setting providing immediate, 24-hour care for children ages 6-13, with some exceptions to age made to keep sibling groups together)

columns.name: FOSTER_CARE
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of DCF "Core" foster home (recruited, trained, licensed and supported by DCF directly)

columns.name: TOTAL_CIP
columns.description: Total number of children in placement on the Month In Care date

columns.name: HOSPITAL
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is in a Hospital setting for either medical or psychiatric reasons

columns.name: INDEPENDENT_LIVING
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of supervised but independent living setting, including Supportive Work, Education and Transition Programs (SWET), or Community Housing Apartment Programs (CHAP)

columns.name: Month_in_Care
columns.description: Observation Date; date the child was residing in an open DCF placement

columns.description: DCF Office responsible for child's case

columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of Group Home, which includes Maternity, Preparing Adolescents for Self-Sufficiency (PASS), Specialized or Therapeutic Group Home settings

columns.name: THERAPEUTIC_FOSTER_CARE
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of Therapeutic foster home (recruited, trained, and supported by a Child-Placing Agency on contract with DCF)

columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is either the Solnit Center North Campus (formerly CT Children's Place) or Solnit Center South Campus (formerly Riverview Psychiatric Hospital)

columns.name: RESIDENTIAL
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is a Residential Treatment Center

columns.name: SHELTER
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is some form of Shelter (a temporary congregate care program that provides short-term care, assessment and a range of clinical and nursing services)

columns.name: SPECIAL_STUDY
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is a Special Study foster home (provider is connected to the child in some fashion, but is not a member of the child's extended family;  in most cases providers are recruited, trained, licensed and supported by DCF directly, though some provide Therapeutic Foster Care but for reporting purposes are included in the Special Study category)

columns.name: RELATIVE_CARE
columns.description: Number of children where placement setting is a Relative Foster home (provider is a member of the child's extended family; in most cases providers are recruited, trained, licensed and supported by DCF directly, though some provide Therapeutic Foster Care but for reporting purposes are included in the Relative Care category)

name: Childhood Lead Poisoning
description: As of January 1, 2009, Connecticut law mandates that medical providers must conduct annual lead screening (i.e., blood lead testing) for each child 9 to 35 months of age. description: Section 8-23 of the Connecticut General Statutes (CGS) requires each municipality to prepare or amend and adopt a plan of conservation and development (POCD) at least once every ten years. In accordance with CGS Sec. 8-23(a), any municipality that does not adopt a POCD every ten years shall be ineligible for discretionary state funding of more than $25,000 unless such prohibition is expressly waived by the OPM Secretary.

columns.name: Reimbursement Rate
columns.description: The percentage of eligible costs the state will reimburse the school district for the construction project. The reimbursement rate is per statutory formula (CGS 102-85a).

columns.name: School Type
columns.description: The type of school for which the grant was awarded (e.g., elementary, middle, or secondary/high).

columns.name: Fiscal Year of Award
columns.description: The fiscal year during which the grant was awarded. The fiscal year begins on July 1 and ends on June 30 of the following year (e.g., FY2023 = July 1, 2022 – June 30, 2023).

columns.name: District Name
columns.description: The official name of the school district or municipality.

columns.name: District Code
columns.description: A numeric code assigned to the school district or municipality. This is used for state-level identification of the school district or municipality.

columns.name: Contract ID
columns.description: A unique identifier assigned to each grant for school construction. This ID helps track specific grant agreements in the CORE-CT system.

columns.name: Estimated Grant
columns.description: The maximum grant funding obligated by the state to the school district for the building project, based on the reimbursement rate and estimated project cost (i.e., estimated project cost x reimbursement rate = estimated grant).

columns.name: Project Status
columns.description: The current status of the school construction project (e.g., audit complete, estimated payment, etc.).

columns.name: School Name
columns.description: The name of the specific school or school facility.

columns.name: Type of Grant
columns.description: The grant category (e.g., priority application, non-priority application).

description: This dataset includes award data for Connecticut's school construction grant program. Data is provided for DAS awarded projects starting July 1, 2019. The data does not represent payments processed to districts. https://data.ct.gov/stories/s/d8g6-ttnf

name: List of Contaminated or Potentially Contaminated Sites
provider: Remediation Division, Bureau of Water Protection and Land Reuse

columns.name: Cleanup Stage
columns.description: Indicates the progress achieved to date to complete investigation and cleanup at a release or property.  Options are "Investigation Started", "Investigation Complete", "Cleanup Started", "Soil Cleanup Complete - GWM Ongoing" (GWM = Groundwater Monitoring), "Cleanup Complete - LTS" (LTS = Long-term Stewardship), "Cleanup Complete - No LTS".

columns.name: Env. Use Restrictions in place
columns.description: Indicates that a cleanup remedy involves the use of Environmental Use Restrictions (EUR) or other institutional controls to communicate how the land can be safely used.  "True" = EUR present, "False" = EUR not present

columns.name: Official Town
columns.description: Municipality name based on 169 CT towns list (non-village)

columns.name: Case Address
columns.description: Address or location associated with the case, may reference a village name

columns.name: Engineered Controls in place
columns.description: This indicates that a cleanup remedy involves the use of engineered control systems to prevent contaminant exposures to human health and/or the environment.

columns.name: REACT Site ID
columns.description: REACT application site record key value

columns.name: REACT Case Number
columns.description: REACT application case record key value

columns.name: Case Program
columns.description: Specific regulatory program directing cleanup requirements

columns.name: Case Name
columns.description: Name assigned to a location undergoing cleanup

columns.name: Case Status
columns.description: Indication whether cleanup obligations remain on the case.  "Open" clean up obligations remain.  "Resolved" cleanup obligations have been “Resolved” with or without the use of institutional and/or engineered controls or other long-term stewardship (“LTS”) obligations.  "Withdrawn" case has been removed from the programatic cleanup program.  "Escalated" the requirements to achieve cleanup has been transitioned to another case.

columns.name: Site Longitude
columns.description: Map location decimal latitude value from related REACT Site ID record

columns.name: Site Latitude
columns.description: Map location decimal longitude value from related REACT Site ID record

columns.name: Agency ID
columns.description: Historical case identification numbers that have been migrated from other tracking systems.  Generally, for remediation cases the Agency ID is the historical “Rem ID,” for LUST Cases the Agency ID is the spill case number or other identification number, for Spill Cases it is the Spill Incident Tracking System Case Number or the Hazconnect Incident Number, for PCB inspection cases the Agency ID is the inspection number identified by DEEP or EPA with the type of inspection being performed and the inspector’s initials.  Some cases may not have an Agency ID due to the age of the case or other reasons.

description: Data to create the List of Contaminated or Potentially Contaminated Sites is based on records from the Connecticut Department of Energy and Environmental Protection (DEEP)’s online REACT portal at: https://deep-react.service.ct.gov/s/ The REACT application tracks environmental cleanups related to contaminant releases and contaminated properties. name: Childhood Lead Poisoning: Prevalence by Town and Blood Lead Level Category
description: As of January 1, 2009, Connecticut law mandates that medical providers must conduct annual lead screening (i.e., blood lead testing) for each child 9 to 35 months of age. Furthermore, the law requires that any child between 36-72 months of age who has not been previously tested must also be tested by the child’s medical provider, regardless of risk. This dataset includes data on the prevalence by town and blood lead level category.

description: This dataset shows the average days supply and daily morphine milligram equivalent (MME) of opioid prescription dispensed. The morphine milligram equivalent is an opioid dosage equivalent to morphine. This measurement is used to determine the abuse and overdose potential, and to set thresholds for prescribing and dispensing opioid pain relievers. The lowest effective dosage is set at less than 50 MME and the highest is greater than 90 MME.

description: This dataset contains statewide CAMA information for parcels in the State of Connecticut. This dataset was created by the GIS Office as required by CGS Sec. 4d-90-92. This dataset is a result of the 2022 data collection effort, which included collecting CAMA data from all municipalities via the Councils of Government.

name: State of CT: Open Expenditures - Ledger Current
description: This data allows citizens to view who received payments from the state for goods or services and how much they received. The data can be explored by searching for specific payee names or by browsing by Government Function. The Open Checkbook app allows the user to drill down from aggregated spending accounts all the way down to each individual payment to a payee.
